name: Gemini 3.5 Flash
description: Google's GA 'frontier workhorse' launched at I/O 2026. Beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic and coding suites (76.2% Terminal-Bench 2.1, 83.6% MCP Atlas) at roughly 4x the speed, with 1M token context. Pricier than past Flash tiers at $1.50/$9.00 per 1M.

evidence.value: 76.2% on terminal/command-line agentic tasks (official claim, beats Gemini 3.1 Pro)
83.6% multi-tool orchestration (official claim, above Gemini 3.1 Pro's 78.2%)

task_accuracy_code.methodology: Official launch benchmarks for agentic coding; vendor-reported, pending broad third-party replication

evidence.value: 57.9% on multi-step financial agent tasks (official claim)
84.2% on chart reasoning (official claim)

task_accuracy_reasoning.methodology: Official agentic and multimodal reasoning benchmarks from launch materials

evidence.value: Positioned as 'frontier workhorse' matching or beating prior Pro tier on agentic suites

task_accuracy_general.methodology: Cross-benchmark comparison against Gemini 3.1 Pro from official launch claims

evidence.value: GA status with ~4x speed of Gemini 3.1 Pro at consistent quality

output_consistency.methodology: Consistency assessment based on GA status and vendor throughput claims

evidence.value: ~4x faster than Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic workloads

latency_p50.methodology: Relative speed claims from official materials; absolute latency varies by workload

evidence.value: 1,048,576 input tokens, 64K output tokens
99.9% uptime (last 90 days, Vertex AI)

uptime.methodology: Historical uptime data from official status page

performance_reliability.notes: Unusual positioning: a Flash-tier model that beats the flagship 3.1 Pro on agentic/coding suites at ~4x speed. Benchmark figures are official claims from launch (2026-05-19); third-party replication still maturing.

evidence.value: Gemini 3.x-generation prompt injection defenses

prompt_injection_resistance.methodology: OWASP LLM01 testing and vendor documentation review

evidence.value: Adversarial robustness consistent with Gemini 3.x family
Paid-tier API data not used for training

data_leakage_prevention.methodology: Privacy policy and API terms review

evidence.value: Configurable multi-category safety filters

output_safety.methodology: Safety filter testing across content categories

evidence.value: Google Cloud security standards, Vertex AI IAM

security.notes: Standard Gemini 3.x-family security posture on Google Cloud infrastructure. High-speed agentic use increases the importance of downstream tool sandboxing.

data_residency.value: Multi-region including EU residency options (Vertex AI)

evidence.value: Regional deployment options via Vertex AI
Paid API data not used for training

data_retention.value: Varies by tier; zero retention available on Vertex AI enterprise

evidence.value: Enterprise zero-retention configurations available
Customer responsible for PII redaction; Cloud DLP integration available
SOC 1/2/3, ISO 27001/27017/27018, GDPR, HIPAA (via Google Cloud)
Zero-retention configuration available for enterprise customers

privacy_compliance.notes: Same Google Cloud compliance envelope as the Pro tier: SOC/ISO certifications, GDPR, HIPAA via Google Cloud, EU residency options.

evidence.value: Thinking traces available, though shallower than Pro-tier extended reasoning
84.2% chart reasoning suggests solid grounding; speed-optimized tiers historically hallucinate slightly more

hallucination_rate.methodology: Benchmark-derived grounding assessment; vendor claims pending independent replication

evidence.value: Regular bias testing and mitigation per AI Principles

bias_fairness.methodology: Bias benchmark evaluation and policy review

evidence.value: Limited independent assessment so far; recent GA release

uncertainty_quantification.methodology: Qualitative assessment; limited data given ~7 weeks since GA

evidence.value: Launch documentation with benchmarks, specs, and pricing
General training description; detailed sources not disclosed
Configurable multi-category safety guardrails

trust_transparency.notes: Solid documentation at launch; ~7 weeks GA as of 2026-07-09. Most benchmark figures remain vendor-reported and independent verification is still accumulating.

evidence.value: RESTful API with streaming, function calling, multimodal, context caching

api_design_quality.methodology: API design and feature completeness review

evidence.value: Unified Gen AI SDKs across Python, Node.js, Go, Java
GA at I/O 2026 with documented model lifecycle
gemini-3.5-flash active with no shutdown date; named designated replacement for gemini-2.0-flash (retired 2026-06-01), gemini-2.5-flash (shutdown 2026-10-16), and gemini-3-flash-preview
Comprehensive Cloud Console and Vertex AI monitoring
Enterprise support tiers with SLAs
Day-one availability across AI Studio, Vertex AI, and Gemini app
Standard commercial terms; enterprise agreements available

operational_excellence.notes: Full Google Cloud operational stack from day one. Pricing ($1.50/$9.00, cached input $0.15) is notably higher than past Flash tiers, narrowing the cost gap to Pro models.

code-generation.notes: 76.2% Terminal-Bench 2.1 and 83.6% MCP Atlas — beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic coding at ~4x speed. Excellent agent-loop economics.

customer-support.notes: Speed plus frontier quality is ideal for high-volume support. Multimodal input handles screenshots.

data-analysis.notes: 84.2% CharXiv chart reasoning and 1M context for large datasets at workhorse cost.

financial-analysis.notes: 57.9% Finance Agent v2 is strong for an agentic suite, but high-stakes analysis still favors Pro-tier reasoning.

research-assistant.notes: 1M context and fast iteration; deepest reasoning tasks still favor Gemini 3.1 Pro.

content-creation.notes: Fast, capable long-form generation for production content pipelines.

education.notes: Low latency suits interactive tutoring; strong multimodal explanations.

strengths: Beats Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic/coding suites: 76.2% Terminal-Bench 2.1, 83.6% MCP Atlas
~4x faster than Gemini 3.1 Pro — strong agent-loop economics
Context caching at $0.15 per 1M cuts repeated-prefix costs dramatically
GA from day one across AI Studio, Vertex AI, and Gemini app
Full Google Cloud compliance envelope (SOC/ISO, GDPR, HIPAA via GCP)
limitations: Pricier than past Flash tiers ($1.50/$9.00 vs historical sub-dollar Flash pricing)
Benchmark figures are official claims; independent replication still accumulating (~7 weeks since GA)
Deepest reasoning tasks still favor Pro-tier models
Under two months of production track record
best_for: High-volume agentic workflows where speed-cost-quality balance matters
Production coding agents with many tool-call iterations
Customer-facing applications needing frontier quality at low latency
Long-context processing on a workhorse budget
not_recommended_for: Ultra-low-cost bulk inference (older Flash/Lite tiers remain cheaper)
Risk-averse deployments requiring long production track records

pricing.notes: Confirmed on the official Gemini API pricing page 2026-07-09. Cached input $0.15 per 1M; batch API 50% discount ($0.75/$4.50). Pricier than past Flash tiers, reflecting 'frontier workhorse' positioning.

metadata.architecture: Speed-optimized multimodal transformer with thinking support
